frame_analyses.description: First frame shows anatomical diagram of female reproductive system with labeled structures (uterus, fallopian tubes, ovaries, cervix, vagina, bladder).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: This is educational anatomy reference material, not a performance video.

frame_analyses.description: Repeat of female reproductive anatomy diagram.
Diagram showing abdominal insufflation with Veress needle insertion into abdominal cavity for CO2 inflation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content: demonstrates pneumoperitoneum setup, not the endoloop task itself.

frame_analyses.description: Continuation of Veress needle and gas insufflation diagram with explanatory text.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup phase for laparoscopy, not task 3 execution.

frame_analyses.description: Further insufflation diagram showing expanded abdominal cavity.
Final insufflation diagram with full abdominal distension.
Diagram titled 'Steps of Tubal Sterilization Using Laparoscopy' showing laparoscope insertion and fishie clip placement on fallopian tubes.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Introduction to tubal sterilization procedure overview; fishie clips are shown, not endoloop.

frame_analyses.description: Diagram showing laparoscopic view of abdomen and pelvic structures with forceps grasping tubes.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational visualization of surgical anatomy during laparoscopy.

frame_analyses.description: Diagram showing laparoscope instrument insertion port and forceps placement.
Diagram showing bilateral fallopian tube visualization with instrument positioning.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Bilateral anatomy and instrument approach demonstration.

frame_analyses.description: Diagram showing clip placement on left fallopian tube.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Fishie clip application on left tube shown.

frame_analyses.description: Diagram showing fishie clip already secured on left tube and preparation for right tube clipping.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Progressive sterilization procedure using clips, not endoloop ligating loop.

frame_analyses.description: Diagram showing both fallopian tubes with fishie clips applied bilaterally.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Bilateral clip application completed; this is a tubal sterilization procedure instructional video, not FLS task 3 endoloop.

frame_analyses.description: Final diagram showing completed tubal sterilization with bilateral fishie clips and text referencing cauterization, cutting, or banding as alternative methods.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational overview of various sterilization techniques including clips and thermal methods.

video_type_assessment: NOT AN FLS TASK 3 PERFORMANCE VIDEO
content_summary: This is a medical educational video series about laparoscopic tubal sterilization procedures. The content covers: (1) female reproductive anatomy, (2) abdominal insufflation technique, and (3) laparoscopic sterilization using fishie clips and alternative methods. The video contains NO performance of the FLS task 3 endoloop ligating loop skill.
cannot_determine: tail_cut_execution — no cutting of loop tail performed
final_loop_security — no endoloop task completion visible
reasoning: The provided video frames contain exclusively instructional diagrams and animated illustrations of female pelvic anatomy and laparoscopic tubal sterilization procedures using fishie clips. The FLS task 3 endoloop ligating loop task is NOT demonstrated or performed anywhere in this sequence. Task 3 requires placement of a pre-tied ligating loop around a foam appendage on a trainer model; this video shows none of that equipment, context, or skill execution. Per the assessment instructions: instructional content with narration, overlays, and educational focus should NOT be scored as a performance task. Since this is purely instructional medical education content with no actual FLS endoloop task execution visible, a performance score cannot be assigned.

demonstrated_techniques: Female reproductive anatomy identification (uterus, fallopian tubes, ovaries, cervix, vagina)
Veress needle insertion technique for abdominal insufflation
Carbon dioxide insufflation for pneumoperitoneum creation
Abdominal cavity distension and visualization preparation
Secondary instrument port placement above pubic hairline
Fishie clip application and placement on fallopian tubes
Alternative sterilization methods: cauterization, cutting, and band application
verbal_cues: Carbon dioxide inflation to move abdominal wall away from intestines
Laparoscope placement for visualization of uterus and fallopian tubes
Fishie clip placement and locking mechanism
Alternative sterilization techniques referenced in text boxes
common_errors_addressed: Improper Veress needle angle or position (shown with correct trajectory)
Poor visualization angle or instrument positioning (shown with optimal laparoscope placement)
teaching_content.educational_context: Medical/surgical education for laparoscopic gynecologic procedures; appropriate for surgical residents, laparoscopy trainees, or medical students learning sterilization procedures.

conclusion: This video is educational material about laparoscopic tubal sterilization using fishie clips and related techniques. It is NOT a performance video of FLS task 3 (endoloop ligating loop). No performance scoring is applicable. If a task 3 endoloop FLS performance video is available, please provide that for evaluation.
